discoveredKeywordsDescription: {count} keywords found across ranked pages and keyword suggestions

dataSourcesRanked: Ranked keywords: found via SERP position checks for your domain
dataSourcesSuggested: Suggested keywords: discovered through keyword research API
noRankTooltip: No ranking position found for this keyword
difficultyNotAvailableTooltip: Difficulty data not available for this keyword
keywordMonitoringDescription: {count} keywords being actively monitored for position changes

serpFetchJobQueued: SERP fetch job queued for {count} keywords

best seo tools

suggestedKeywordsInfo: Suggested keywords are based on your top-ranking discovered keywords with high search volume. You can edit the list before adding.
pleaseEnterKeyword: Please enter at least one keyword
noSuggestionsAvailable: No suggestions available — try fetching SERP data first
suggestedKeywords: Suggested {count} keywords based on your rankings
validationLooksLikeUrl: Looks like a URL, not a keyword
validationLooksLikeDomain: Looks like a domain, not a keyword
allKeywordsInvalid: All {count} keywords were invalid. Check format and try again.
addedKeywordsDetailed: Added {added} keywords ({skipped} duplicates skipped, {invalid} invalid filtered)
pleaseSelectCompetitor: Please select at least one competitor
reportWaitingToProcess: Report is waiting to be processed
positionDistributionDescription: How your keywords are distributed across SERP positions. More keywords in top 1-3 means stronger visibility.
createGroupsToSeePerformance: Create keyword groups to see performance comparison
groupPerformanceDescription: Average ranking position over time for each keyword group. Track which groups are improving or declining.
difficultyDistributionDescription: {total} keywords grouped by ranking difficulty. Focus on easy and moderate tiers for quicker wins.
searchIntentDistributionDescription: Breakdown of keywords by user search intent — informational, commercial, transactional, or navigational.
serpFeaturesTooltip: SERP features are special result types Google shows beyond the standard blue links, such as featured snippets, image packs, and knowledge panels. Winning these features can dramatically increase your visibility and click-through rate, even if you don't rank #1 organically.
serpFeaturesSubtitle: These special Google result types appear for your keywords. Hover over each to learn how to capture them.
serpFeatureUnknownInfo: This SERP feature appears for {count} of your keywords at an average position of #{position}.
keywordMapDescription: Volume vs Difficulty — bubble size = ETV, color = intent
noKeywordDataForBubbleChart: No keyword data available for bubble chart
cannibalizationDescription: URLs ranking for multiple keywords — potential cannibalization
eachUrlRanksUnique: Each URL ranks for a unique keyword set
topicClusterWithGaps: Topic cluster with {count} content gap opportunities
estTrafficTooltip: Estimated monthly visits if you ranked for these keywords (~30% CTR assumed)
totalVolumeTooltip: Combined monthly search volume across all keywords in this cluster
avgScoreTooltip: Average opportunity score: higher means better ROI potential
avgDifficultyTooltip: Average keyword difficulty (0-100). Lower is easier to rank for
strategyLowCompetitionLarge: This is a low-competition cluster with {gapCount} easy keywords. Create a pillar page covering "{topic}" broadly, then interlink {gapCount} supporting articles targeting each keyword. Quick wins with high topical authority potential.
strategyLowDifficulty: Low difficulty keywords — create targeted content for each. Start with the highest-volume keywords and work down. Internal linking between pieces will boost rankings.
strategyModerateHighVolume: Moderate difficulty with strong search volume ({volume}/mo). Build a comprehensive content hub: one authority pillar page + supporting cluster articles. Focus on long-tail variations first to build momentum.
strategyModerate: Moderate competition. Prioritize keywords with the highest opportunity scores. Create detailed, well-researched content and ensure strong internal linking between related pages.
strategyHighValue: High-value cluster despite competition. Build topical authority gradually: start with easier long-tail keywords, create expert-level content, and pursue backlinks. The traffic potential justifies the investment.
strategyCompetitive: Competitive cluster — approach strategically. Identify the 2-3 easiest keywords to start with, build authority content, then tackle harder terms as your domain gains strength in this topic.
topicClustersTooltip: Groups related content gap keywords by shared topics. Targeting entire clusters helps build topical authority and rank for multiple related terms.
topicClustersDescription: Related content gap keywords grouped by topic. Targeting entire clusters helps build topical authority and rank for multiple related terms at once.
noClustersFound: No clusters found. Run content gap analysis first.
tooltipTopicDescription: Cluster name derived from the most common meaningful word across grouped keywords.
tooltipKeywordsDescription: Number of content gap keywords grouped under this topic cluster.
tooltipAvgScoreDescription: Average opportunity score (0-100) across all keywords in the cluster. Higher = better ROI potential.
tooltipTotalVolumeDescription: Combined monthly search volume for all keywords in this cluster.
tooltipAvgDifficultyDescription: Average keyword difficulty (0-100) for the cluster. Lower = easier to rank for.
tooltipEstTrafficDescription: Estimated monthly visits if you ranked for all keywords in this cluster (~30% CTR assumed).
noClustersMatchFilters: No clusters match your filters. Try adjusting them.
checkBackAfterRankingUpdate: Check back after the first ranking update
noDiscoveredKeywordsDesc: Fetch keyword visibility data from DataForSEO to discover what keywords this domain ranks for
refreshToFetchHistory: Click "Refresh Keywords" to fetch visibility history
serpFeaturesAppearAfterChecks: SERP features will appear here after keyword position checks
percentageOfKeywordsShowingFeature: Percentage of keywords showing each feature
serpFeaturesExplanation: SERP features are special elements that appear in Google search results, such as featured snippets, image packs, and local results. Tracking these helps identify opportunities to appear in enhanced search results.
noSerpFeaturesDataAvailable: No SERP features data available yet. Features will be tracked during keyword position checks.
needAtLeast10Days: Need at least 10 days of historical data
serpFeatureDescFeaturedSnippet: A summary answer displayed above organic results (position 0). Captures 35-50% of clicks for the query and massively boosts brand authority.
serpFeatureDescPeopleAlsoAsk: An expandable FAQ box showing related questions. Appears in 40-65% of searches. Each expanded answer links back to a source page, providing additional visibility.
serpFeatureDescKnowledgePanel: An information box on the right side of SERP showing key facts about entities (brands, people, places). Signals Google recognizes you as an authority.
serpFeatureDescLocalPack: A map with 3 local business results. Dominates clicks for local intent queries ("near me", city-based searches). Critical for businesses serving specific geographic areas.
serpFeatureDescImagePack: A row of image thumbnails in search results. Clicking opens Google Images with a link to the source page. Provides an additional entry point to your content.
serpFeatureDescVideoResults: Video thumbnails (usually from YouTube) embedded in search results. Video results get 41% higher CTR than plain text results and are increasingly favored by Google.
serpFeatureDescShoppingResults: Product listings with images, prices, and store names. Appears for commercial/transactional queries. Drives high-intent traffic directly to product pages.
serpFeatureDescSitelinks: Additional links below your main result showing key pages on your site. Takes up more SERP real estate and helps users navigate directly to relevant sections.
serpFeatureDescTopStories: A carousel of recent news articles related to the query. Provides massive short-term traffic spikes for timely content. Requires being indexed as a news source.
serpFeatureDescRelatedSearches: Suggested related queries at the bottom of SERP. These are content ideas straight from Google showing what users also search for around your topic.
serpFeatureDescReviewStars: Star ratings displayed in search results (1-5 stars). Results with review stars see 20-35% higher CTR. Builds trust and stands out visually in SERP.
serpFeatureDescFaqRichResult: Expandable FAQ answers displayed directly in SERP under your listing. Increases your result's visual size and provides immediate answers that build trust.
serpFeatureDescCarousel: A horizontal scrollable list of results, often showing entities, products, or related topics. Provides visual browsing experience directly on SERP.
serpFeatureDescAnswerBox: A direct answer to a specific question, displayed prominently above results. Similar to featured snippets but for factual queries (calculations, conversions, definitions).
serpFeatureDescPaidAds: Google Ads appearing above or below organic results. When ads dominate a SERP, organic CTR drops significantly. Indicates high commercial value of the keyword.
serpFeatureDescTwitter: Recent tweets/posts from X (Twitter) shown in a carousel. Appears for trending topics, brands, and public figures. Provides real-time social proof.
serpFeatureDescJobs: Job listings displayed directly in search results. Appears for job-related queries. Can drive qualified candidate traffic directly from SERP.
serpFeatureDescRecipes: Rich recipe cards with images, cooking time, ratings, and calories. Dominates food-related queries and provides a very high CTR due to visual appeal.
serpFeatureOptFeaturedSnippet: Structure content with clear Q&A format, use headers that match the query, provide concise 40-60 word answers in a paragraph, list, or table directly below the heading.
serpFeatureOptPeopleAlsoAsk: Add an FAQ section to your page. Answer each related question concisely (40-50 words) under an H2/H3, then expand with detail. Use schema markup (FAQPage) to reinforce.
serpFeatureOptKnowledgePanel: Claim your Google Business Profile, ensure consistent NAP data across directories, use Organization/Person schema markup, and build Wikipedia/Wikidata presence.
serpFeatureOptLocalPack: Optimize Google Business Profile with accurate info, collect reviews (aim for 4.5+ stars), ensure NAP consistency, add local schema markup, create location-specific landing pages.
serpFeatureOptImagePack: Use original, high-quality images. Add descriptive filenames and alt text with target keywords. Implement image sitemap. Use WebP format and proper image dimensions.
serpFeatureOptVideoResults: Create YouTube videos for target keywords. Add timestamps, closed captions, and VideoObject schema. Embed relevant videos on your pages. Optimize video titles and descriptions.
serpFeatureOptShoppingResults: Set up Google Merchant Center, submit product feed with accurate pricing and availability, use Product schema, add competitive pricing and high-quality product images.
serpFeatureOptSitelinks: Maintain clear site structure with descriptive navigation. Use internal linking. Add breadcrumb schema. Ensure your pages have unique, descriptive titles and meta descriptions.
serpFeatureOptTopStories: Publish timely, newsworthy content. Register with Google News. Use Article/NewsArticle schema. Ensure fast page load and AMP compatibility. Publish frequently.
serpFeatureOptRelatedSearches: Use these as content ideas. Create content targeting related queries. Add internal links between related topic pages to build topical authority.
serpFeatureOptReviewStars: Implement Review/AggregateRating schema. Collect genuine customer reviews. Display reviews on product and service pages. Aim for 4+ star average.
serpFeatureOptFaqRichResult: Add FAQPage schema markup to pages with FAQ sections. Each Q&A should be concise but helpful. Keep answers under 300 characters for best display.
serpFeatureOptCarousel: Use structured data (ItemList, Product, or Event schema). Create comprehensive, well-organized content around the topic. Ensure your entities are well-defined.
serpFeatureOptAnswerBox: Provide clear, factual answers. Use definition lists, tables, and structured formats. Target "what is", "how to", and question-based queries with concise answers.
serpFeatureOptPaidAds: For keywords with heavy ad presence, focus on featured snippets and other rich results to stand out. Consider running ads for the most competitive terms.
serpFeatureOptTwitter: Maintain an active X/Twitter presence. Post about topics you want to rank for. Use relevant hashtags. Engage with trending conversations in your niche.
serpFeatureOptJobs: Use JobPosting schema markup. Post jobs on Google for Jobs. Include salary range, location, and job type in structured data.
serpFeatureOptRecipes: Use Recipe schema with all fields (cook time, ingredients, nutrition, images). Add step-by-step instructions with photos. Include ratings and reviews.
groupManagementDescription: Organize your keywords into custom groups for better analysis
noGroupsYet: No groups created yet. Create your first group above.
deleteGroupDescription: This will remove {count} keywords from this group. The keywords themselves will not be deleted.
selectAtLeastOneUrl: Please select at least one URL to scan
removeCompetitorConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove this competitor? It will be hidden from tracking.
removeCompetitorsConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove {count} competitor(s)?
refreshConfirmDescription: This will queue a position check for {count} keywords.
serpConfirmDescription: This will queue SERP data fetch for {count} keywords.
limitBulkCapExceeded: Max {limit} keywords per bulk action ({count} selected)
bulkDeleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete {count} keywords? This action cannot be undone.
bulkDeleteWarning: All position history data for these keywords will also be deleted.
bulkMoveToGroupDescription: Move {count} keywords to the selected group
